sentences.text: In MCCs/E-Houses specifically: Bucket-level work often carries a different (sometimes higher) incident energy than the main bus due to fault current and clearing time differences — always check the bucket-specific label, not just the lineup's main label.
Jurisdiction note: MSHA (30 CFR Part 56/57) governs electrical safety at mine sites, not OSHA, and MSHA's own regulations have not formally adopted NFPA 70E as a federal requirement.

That said, your site's electrical safety program is what actually governs your day-to-day work — and at Freeport-McMoRan sites, NFPA 70E compliance is required as part of that program, and this training itself is what qualifies you to enter MCC rooms and e-houses at all.
Know your site's specific qualified-person and PPE category rules before you start; "MSHA doesn't require it" is not the same as "my site doesn't require it."
